$comment: minimumDependencyAge (Deno 2.9+ default: 24h) blocks resolving any npm/JSR version published in the trailing window — a supply-chain guard against freshly-published (possibly compromised) releases. Exempted here: the whole @bounded-systems scope, since it's first-party/org-controlled — the risk the guard exists for doesn't apply to our own packages, and the default 24h otherwise stalls check-contrast.yml every time baobab (or any sibling package) cuts a release. Non-bounded-systems deps keep the full 24h default. See bounded-systems/mint#<issue> for the batch-rollout problem this papers over (this list is a hand-maintained snapshot, not auto-generated) and https://docs.deno.com/runtime/packages/supply_chain/ for the mechanism itself.
